Petition For Reimbursement Of Funds; Declaration Of June Shimabuku In Support Of Petition
Off Calendar Jul-08-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Frangos for June Shimabuku. Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Frangos stated that he got a letter from Daniel, that Daniel had no objection to the Mother being paid, that they have a stipulation between all of the Family Heirs regarding the property, and that the properties are worthless. Mr. Ferdon stated that he objects to June's accounting, that he objected to anyone being paid, that he received no direction as to how to proceed with the Kern County property and the property in Mexico, and that he was not being apprised of the status of the properties or the personal property. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-07-2009 Continued To Jan-04-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Grant Tomioka for George Shimabuku.

The Court stated that it would accept the copies of the bank statements in lieu of the originals, that there was an issue with the window of time between the date of death of George Shimabuku up to the appointment of the Public Administrator and that more account information was needed. Mr. Tomioka asked to waive that account period stating that no one was administering the estate, that there were no disbursments, that he had some bank statements and that there was minimal interest earned on the accounts. The Court stated that notice had to be given to all entitled Parties. The matter was continued to January 4, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Tomioka to get a waiver of notice from Franchesca Callejo (Counsel for the Minor) for the First Amended Account, for waiver of the missing account period from all entitled Parties or for status on filing of the Second Supplemental Account covering from May 20, 2007 through March 26, 2009.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA).

There being no overbids, the sale was confirmed at $598,000.00 (granted subject to a written order). The bond is waived. The SFPA will submit the order.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Bank Records
Off Calendar Jul-15-2009 Continued To Sep-23-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Grant Tomioka for George Shimabuku. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA").

Mr. Ferdon stated that he sent a letter to the bank last week requesting statements on the estate account and that the SFPA was going to enter the house and change the locks as the keys were never turned over. Mr. Tomioka stated that he received a hand full of statements, that he did not receive any cancelled checks and that no check registry was found. In addition, Mr. Tomioka stated that $500.00 to $1,000.00 was spent per month for an eight month period and that he would be able to put together an accounting once he received the estate account bank records. The matter was continued to September 23, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR#7489. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F.Smith.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-15-2009 Continued To Sep-23-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-23-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bryan Smith for and with June Shimabuku. Evyn Shomer for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA") specially appearing for Jonathan Ferdon.

Mr. Smith apologized to the Court, stated that there was a miscommunication between him and the Attorney that appeared on his behalf at the last hearing and that it was his understanding that the Public Administrator had been appointed. In addition, he stated that he had turned over all of the documents to Mr. Tomioka and that June no longer had standing to request documents from the banks. The Order To Show Cause was taken off calendar. The Parties were directed to confer in the hallway and figure out what documents had been provided or still needed.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Bank Records
Off Calendar May-13-2009 Continued To Jul-15-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Grant Tomioka for George Shimabuku. June Shimabuku in Propria Persona.

The Parties stated that they had not heard from Brian Smith. Ms. Shimabuku stated that she turned over the documents to Mr. Smith on April 22, 2009. An Order to Show Cause will be issued to Brian Smith to show cause as to why he should not be held in contempt or have sanctions imposed as an alternative to contempt for failure to appear and for violation of the Court order. The return date is June 23, 2009 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204. Mr. Tomioka stated that he filed the accounting on April 21, 2009. Mr. Ferdon stated that he had received nothing, that he had no information about the condition of the property, what to do with the property and that Mr. Tomioka's document was not an accounting. The Parties were directed to meet outside of the Courtroom and discuss a plan on how to proceed. Upon returning to the Courtroom, Mr. Ferdon requested a 60 day continuance for a status report on the bank records and stated that instructions as to the real property was to sell. The matter was continued to July 15, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Ferdon will submit an order after hearing.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR#7077. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Aquino.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Bank Records
Off Calendar Mar-17-2009 Continued To May-13-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Jason Honaker specially appeared for Bryan Smith for and with June Shimabuku.

Mr. Honaker reported that Ms. Shimabuku was able to obtain a $10,000.00 bond and stated that $40,000.00 could be blocked. The Court will remove June Shimabuku as the Administrator of the estate and appoint the San Francisco Public Adminstrator as the Successor Administrator. Mr. Ferdon expressed concern about the SFPA taking over the estate without an accounting being filed, stated that liability starts today and that he had no information on the status of the assets, the property or the taxes. The Court will order Grant Tomioka to appear on May 13, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Tomioka is ordered to file an accounting based on the information he has even if it is incomplete. The Court also orders Bryan Smith and June Shimabuku to appear on May 13, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with a report on all of the assets. Ms. Shimabuku is ordered to turn over all bank statements to Mr. Smith. Mr. Smith is ordered to turn over all bank statements to Mr. Tomioka forthwith. Mr. Ferdon will prepare an order. The matter was continued to May 13, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Paula Shi, CSR#13358. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Off Calendar Mar-03-2009 Continued To Mar-17-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bryan Smith for and with June Shimabuku.

Mr. Smith stated that the accounting was to be filed by Mr. Tomioka, that his client (June Shimabuku) had provided Mr. Tomioka with bank statements, but the statements didn't cover the period of time Mr. Tomioka needed. In addition, he stated that they were not in a place to be able to get the paperwork from the banks, that there was a delay in getting the letters issued, that his client had issues with getting bonded, and that if she does not get bonded she would resign. Ms. Shimabuku stated that she turned over two years of bank statements. Mr. Smith stated that it appeared that Mr. Tomioka has some gaps. The Court continued the matter to March 17, 2009 and ordered that the bond be filed within two weeks.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy S. Neu.

Off Calendar Jan-27-2009 Continued To Mar-03-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Brian Smith for June Shimabuku.

Mr. Smith stated that he conferred with Mr. Ferdon and that they were agreeable to a 30 day continuance. In addition, he stated that his Client was in the process of obtaining the bond, that his Client located the bank statements and was in the process of providing them to Mr. Tomioka and that he would have the accounting on file within 30 days. Mr. Ferdon expressed concern with the matter going on for a long time and stated that he would charge the estate for his time. The matter was continued to March 3, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the bond and for status on the accounting by Mr. Tomioka. The Court ordered Mr. Smith to turn over all banks statements to Mr. Tomioka by January 30, 2009 and ordered that Mr. Tomioka's accounting was to be filed by March 3, 2009.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Shannon.

Mr. Smith requested a continuance stating that his Client's financial knowledge had to come from Mr. Tomioka's client (Dennis Shimabuku), as Dennis had knowledge as to where the financial institutions were. The matter was continued to January 27, 2009 for the issuance of the bond and for the issuance of letters of administration for June Shimabuku as well as turning over of bank records by June Shimabuku to Grant Tomioka.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA). Grant Tomioka for George Shimabuku. Bryan Smith for June Shimabuku.

Mr. Smith stated that his Client had been appointed Guardian Ad Litem of the Minor's estate. The Court informed Mr. Ferdon that his services were no longer needed, granted the appointment of June Shimabuku with limited authority and set the bond at $50,000.00, (order for the same signed in open court). George Shimabuku's accounting was set for February 17, 2009.

Off Calendar Jun-16-2008 Continued To Aug-25-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gerald Smith for the Petitioner. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA"). Grant Tomioka for the deceased Former Administrator.

Mr. Smith requested a continuance stating that he needed to re-submit the petition in San Mateo for June to be appointed as the Guardian of the estate. Mr. Tomioka had no objection to the continuance, stated that his client did not have access to all the bank statements and stated that his client was willing to step forward. Mr. Ferdon stated that if the SFPA got involved, an accounting from Dennis was needed and that the 2 peices of real property needed attention and accountability. The Petition of Appointment of Successor Administrator was continued to August 25, 2008. Status for Dennis Shimabuku's accounting for George is also set for August 25, 2008.
